senses.gloss: protective coverings; protective war equipment, armor
protective coverings; an armor plate held in hand, a shield
protective coverings; something that resembles or is used like a shield (such as human shields)
protective coverings; a protective shell or covering (as of a tortoise), armature
to put armor on, to armor

etymology.text: From the root د ر ع (d r ʕ). Compare Ge'ez ድርዕ (dərʿ, “coat of mail, harness”), Tigre ደርዕ (därʿ), Tigrinya ድርዒ (dərʿi), Amharic ዲር (dir), ድር (dər), Soqotri daraḥ (“to put on (a garment)”). Nöldeke thinks that the Arabic is borrowed from Ge'ez.
